title: Unit 1 Checkpoint Quiz — Present Tense & Stem-Changers

questions.prompt: What is the tú form of 'querer'?
questions.explanation: 'Querer' is an e→ie stem-changer. The tú form is 'quieres'.

questions.prompt: Which verb has an irregular yo form 'hago'?
questions.explanation: 'Hacer' (to do/make) has the irregular yo form 'hago'.

questions.prompt: Fill in the blank: Nosotros ___ en México. (vivir)
questions.explanation: Nosotros forms of -IR verbs end in -imos: vivimos. Also note that nosotros is NOT affected by stem changes.

questions.prompt: Which is the correct yo form of 'salir'?
questions.explanation: 'Salir' has an irregular yo form: salgo. Remember: go-verbs add -go in the yo form.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'She can come tomorrow' in Spanish?
questions.explanation: 'Poder' is o→ue. The ella form is 'puede'.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to know a person or place'?
questions.explanation: 'Conocer' means to know a person or place. 'Saber' means to know a fact or skill.

questions.prompt: Which form of 'pedir' is correct for 'ellos'?
questions.explanation: 'Pedir' is an e→i stem-changer. The ellos form is 'piden' — stem changes to 'pid-'.

questions.prompt: In Mexican Spanish, 'ahorita' most commonly means:
questions.explanation: 'Ahorita' is a uniquely Mexican expression that can mean right now, in a little while, or soon — the exact meaning depends on context and tone.
